title: C Values, Integer Conversions, and Size Arithmetic · C Contract Brief: Bitint C23 Boundary
summary: A binary record parser receives attacker-controlled counts that cross signedness and multiplication boundaries before allocation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem bitint c23 boundary increment in checked size and conversion policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate C23 _BitInt and implementation limits without assuming a novel width improves ABI portability.
Reject overflowing addition, multiplication, alignment, and growth calculations before allocation using portable checked patterns or C23 checked arithmetic.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Bitint C23 Boundary · Theory Bitint C23 Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8731. Produce bounded C evidence to reject a size calculation that exceeds its bound; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Evaluate C23 _BitInt and implementation limits without assuming a novel width improves ABI portability.
